title: quercus spicata var. placentaria
intro: Quercus spicata var. placentaria, also known as the desert live oak, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Quercus genus, primarily native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Quercus spicata var. placentaria can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
